text.p: Owu was a prosperous city headed by a King Akinjobi who's wife was Erelu. they had a son called dejumo, before the birth of dejumo chief priest had earlier warned that erelu will give birth to a baby boy whom will be after his birth, due to the calamity he would bring to the land of owu.

But Erelu being kind-hearted decided to keep this child and brought him up to manhood. later dejumo went to war against the people of Ile Ife wer where the killed the people and took away their bodies of war, and sold some of the people into slavery. But On the cause of war dejumo came accross a woman by the name Inyuloye whom he Immediately falled in love with, but this woman was the wife of an artist called Okunade blessed by obatala: the god of creativity.

After the war okunade veng been vengefull joined the army and rose to the rank of a maye, 2 characters and led the people allied forces consisting of Ile Ife, oyo, and Ijebu ode, to war against the people of owu.

As the launched an ambush against the people of owu whom were visited by fire out-break, opened the gate of the city and then the allied forces gained access Into the city and killed all the heirs of owu, and distroyed the city.
Humour is the creation of laughter in the minds of the audience.
It was used in the book The ol The ar "Arms and the man" by Benard Shaw

Humor was Induced as the bluntchili whom was reffered to as the man was as he jumped jumped in into raina's bed-chember. As they disscussed issues together raina sat upon his gun which he used to threaten her in order not to alarm the household. As she sat on the gun she realises that it was a gun and then he stood up in fear but the man told him that the gun was not loaded with bullet rather it was loaded with chocolate.

When the man came back to return the coat to the house raina, cathine ordered Nicola to bring out his luggage for him. Suddenly raina came down from the upstairs and saw the man she shouted "The sol chocolate cream soldier". petkoff was suprised as asked how he knew this man she used Nicola as a comouflage for her over excitement on seeing the man petkoff embarrassed Nicola and made jest of him which induced laughter in the play as petkoff reffered to him as a "portar handed monkey".

The poem the boy on a swing written by Shalit oswald starts with the setence Slowly he moves to and fro to and fro, noting the the movement of a boy "he" on a swing. This book harbours a hidden agenda , a boy whom is on a swing should be happy but towar going towards the second stanza 2 characters the full meaning of the been on a swing really exposed of been in cofususion and poverty was also expressed the writer says "His blue shirt billows in the breeze like a tattered kite".

This boys shirt was blue and tattered expressing poverty and suffering In stanza 3 the cofusion was then well expressed as the more he thought the more confused he gets; East turns west North becomes south this expressed more confusion in the boys head
In the last stanza the boy was noticed to have stood up aganst the challenges of his was facing as he asked Mother were did I come from why was my father jailed.
Meanwhile this was expressing man's Inhumanity to man as this was the same problem that faced the black's during the colonial days in Southafrica.
A Woman in her prime written by Asare Konadu , was set in brenhoma, in ghana
In this village lived a woman called Adwoa pokuwa whom was believed to have been barren

These people believed some much in spirit and there god tano. They believed that tano every sixth week of ever every six week they visited tano in thanks given to him , they also believed that he aided grants people's which wish through their ancestors.
They also believed in their dead fathers and served them as their god this was the reason why they appeased their gods in chapter five of the book, because a tree caught fire in the village

The people so much believed in tano Individual and reffered to it as the supreme god. This was the reason why tano after the sacrifice tano didn't grant them their wish, but they: kwak Fordwuo kwadwuo had persisted that they visited tano despite failing to grant them their wish, her mother also urged her to give thanks to tano visit tano despite the futile result of the first sacifice
Adwoa pokuwa also believed in the existence of gods she thought that her grand father and mother were responsible for the dismay she was facing
symb In the book symbolism was used as by ernest hemmingway as 1 word follows:
Santiago: was is the hew in this book symbolised Jesus Christ as the sores in his hand expressed it, even his lonelness and suffering in the sea made it so. he was destined to be poor.
Sea: The represents the whole world in which santiago was left alone without a companion
Manolin: He symbolized the disciples of christ as he showed love and care before Santiago went into the sea
Shark: The sharks symbolised the the cruel challenges of life which Santiago defeated

The marlin: The marlin 4 characters symbolised the evidence that the man really went into the sea and caught something very big, which would have made him rich both unfortunately 2 characters he came out after the struggling in the sea with a carcass which symbolised the greatness he would have made if he had came out with the fish
